items.headline: Claude in Chrome leaves beta — it's now available to everyone on a paid plan
items.summary: Anthropic flipped Claude in Chrome to "generally available" on July 1. It's a browser extension that lets Claude work right inside your Chrome tabs — reading a page, filling in a form, or pulling details out of what's on screen — instead of you copying and pasting into a separate chat window. It had been in invite-only beta since April; now anyone on a paid Claude plan (Pro, Max, Team, or Enterprise) can just turn it on.
items.why_it_matters: This is one of the most 'show, don't tell' AI features around for a small business owner: instead of babysitting a chatbot in another tab, you let Claude do busywork where you already are — comparing prices across open tabs, pulling info off a booking page, or filling a repetitive web form. It's exactly the kind of everyday AI help that's easy to demo and easy to actually use.

opportunity.action_note: On your Pro or Max plan this week, install the Claude in Chrome extension and run it on one real task (pull details off a page or fill a web form), then record a Saturday demo showing what it nailed and where you had to step in.
opportunity.angle: I let Claude drive my Chrome for a week — here is what it nailed and where I grabbed the wheel back

categories.title: Tools & Tips for Creators and Small Biz
